{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_0","type":"agent_message","text":"Break the import from `src/domain/billing.ts` to `src/presentation/invoice-view.ts`.

Why:

- It is the import that creates both the circular dependency and the explicit layer violation.
- `domain` should not depend on `presentation`; presentation can depend on domain, but not the reverse.
- Breaking this edge preserves the intended architecture better than removing an application-to-domain or presentation-to-application dependency.
- Runtime cycle risk is highest when domain code pulls in UI/view code, because presentation modules often execute rendering/setup code at module load time.

The cycle is:

```text
src/application/billing-flow.ts
 -> src/domain/billing.ts
 -> src/presentation/invoice-view.ts
 -> src/application/billing-flow.ts
```

Recommended fix: move whatever `billing.ts` needs from `invoice-view.ts` into a neutral/domain-owned module, or invert the dependency with a domain-owned interface/callback. If the dependency is only TypeScript types, change it to `import type` so it does not create a runtime edge."}}
